recalls.Description: DR Power Equipment has expanded its recall of walk-behind and tow-behind leaf vacuums due to a potential laceration hazard. The model numbers affected include various configurations of both vacuum types, identifiable by the 'DR' logo on the motor housing. Users should check the model number located on the unit for identification. Loose parts or debris can cause materials to eject, posing risks to users and bystanders.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of DR Power Equipment Leaf Vacuums Due to Laceration Risk; New Repair Kit Available
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DR Power LLP at 877-221-4038 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@drpower.com, or visit https://www.drpower.com/LLV-FreeRepairKit. More information is also available at https://www.drpower.com/ under 'Product Recalls'.

Products.Name: DR Power Walk-Behind Leaf Vacuum
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 60,250 (In addition, about 1,023 were sold in Canada) DR Power Equipment previously recalled 57,200 leaf blowers and vacuums in May 2024. New models of leaf vacuums have been added to this recall

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Equipment walk-behind leaf vacuum
Recalled DR Power Equipment walk-behind leaf vacuum
Recalled DR Power Equipment tow-behind leaf vacuum
Recalled DR Power Equipment tow-behind leaf vacuum

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 46 reports of loose pieces being ejected from the units. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Available at independent power equipment retailers nationwide and online at drpower.com from September 2014 through June 2025, priced between $1,300 and $4,000.

Distributors.Name: DR Power Equipment, of South Burlington, Vermont

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to ejected debris or loose parts

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit with metal outer shield and installation instructions

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power Equipment expands recall of leaf vacuums due to laceration risks. New repair kit available. Check model numbers for safety.

recalls.Description:
Positec is recalling several electric chainsaws and pole saws due to a potential laceration hazard. The affected models include the Hyper Tough 9 Amp Electric Corded 14" Chainsaw, Worx 8 Amp 14" Electric Chainsaw, Worx 8 Amp Electric Pole Saw, and Portland 9 Amp 14" Chainsaw. Each product features distinct colors and branding, such as the Hyper Tough model in black with red accents and the Worx models in orange and black. Specific serial number ranges and item numbers are included in this recall to help identify the affected units.

Consumers are advised to cease using these products immediately. Positec will provide a pre-paid shipping label and a new box for the return of the recalled items at no cost. Once the recalled product is returned, customers will receive a free replacement chainsaw or pole saw. This recall affects items sold at various retailers between September 2024 and March 2025.
recalls.Title: Positec Recalls Electric Chainsaws and Pole Saws Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Positec at 800-889-0129 Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET or visit https://www.trane.com/residential/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/.

Products.Name: Hyper Tough 9 Amp Electric Corded 14" Chainsaw
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 49,000 (In addition, about 3,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Hyper Tough 9 Amp Electric Corded 14" Chainsaw
Recalled Worx 8 Amp 14" Electric Chainsaw with location of Item No. and Serial No.
Recalled Worx 8 Amp Electric Pole Saw with location of Item No. and Serial No.
Recalled Portland 9 Amp 14" Chainsaw with location of UPC, Serial No. and SKU

Importers.Name: Positec Tool Corporation, of Charlotte, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to potential switch failure

Remedies.Name: Free replacement chainsaw or pole saw after returning the recalled product

recalls.metaDescription: Positec recalls electric chainsaws and pole saws due to a laceration hazard. Stop using the products and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: DR Power has issued a recall for its 62-volt 5.0 Ah LiPRO rechargeable lithium-ion battery packs, which are used with various battery-powered equipment like lawnmowers and trimmers. The recall affects stand-alone battery packs identified by model number 414240, as well as removable battery packs that come with certain DR Power outdoor products, including models 524340, SB11022XEN, T4X3026XEN, T4X3062XENR, CE75021XEN0, and CE77021XEN0. The model number for the battery pack can be found on its label, along with the LiPRO and DR Power logos on the packaging. Proper disposal of these batteries is crucial to prevent fire hazards, and they should not be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins.
recalls.Title: DR Power Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery Packs Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DR Power at 800-454-8643 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@drpower.com, or visit https://drpower.com/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: DR Power LiPRO 5.0 Ah Lithium-Ion Battery Packs

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Model 414240 Battery Pack (5 Ah)
DR Power Model 524340, DR Trimmer Mower
DR Power Model SB11022XEN, DR Pulse 62V Snow Thrower
DR Power Model T4X3062XEN, DR Trimmer Pulse
DR Power Model CE75021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21"
DR Power Model CE77021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21" SP

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of incidents of fire and/or overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Ningbo New Team Import & Export Co. Ltd. ("Lera"), of China

Retailers.Name: Available at home improvement and hardware stores nationwide, as well as online at DRPower.com and CountryHomeProducts.com from April 2018 through July 2024, priced between $300 and $700 for equipment including battery packs, and $250 for individual battery packs.

Importers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential short-circuiting and ignition.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the battery packs immediately and contact DR Power for a pro-rated refund. Proof of destruction must be submitted via photographs as per the instructions on the recall website.

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls lithium-ion battery packs due to fire hazards. Learn how to safely dispose and claim your refund.

recalls.Description: TTI Outdoor Power Equipment has issued a recall for the RYOBI 40V 24-Inch Cordless Hedge Trimmers, specifically models RY40620VNM, RY40602VNM, and RY40602BTLVNM. These trimmers have serial numbers ranging from LT21091D180001 to LT22365D060025 and RG23125N250001 to RG24252D101110, which can be found on the data plate located on the bottom of the device. The trimmers pose a laceration hazard as the blade can activate unexpectedly when the safety or trigger control is pressed individually instead of simultaneously. Consumers are advised to stop using the trimmers immediately.
recalls.Title: RYOBI 40V 24-Inch Cordless Hedge Trimmers Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach TTI Outdoor Power Equipment at 800-597-9624 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.ryobitools.com/recall for more details.

Products.Name: RYOBI 40V 24-Inch Cordless Hedge Trimmers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 113,000 (In addition, about 15,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled RYOBI 40V 24" Hedge Trimmer
Location of the data plate

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 27 reports of the blade activating after pressing just one of the controls, including 16 reports of injuries that resulted in minor and some severe lacerations.

Retailers.Name: Available at Home Depot and Direct Tools Factory Outlet stores nationwide and online at www.homedepot.com and www.directtoolsoutlet.com from March 2021 to January 2025, priced between $90 and $160.

Importers.Name: TI Outdoor Power Equipment Inc., of Anderson, South Carolina

Hazards.Name: Unexpected activation of the blade posing a laceration hazard.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the trimmers and check if yours is affected by visiting https://www.ryobitools.com/recall. Return the recalled trimmer to TTI Outdoor Power Equipment for a free replacement. No proof of purchase is needed.

recalls.metaDescription: RYOBI recalls 40V 24-Inch Cordless Hedge Trimmers due to laceration risk. Check if your trimmer is affected and get a free replacement.

recalls.Description: DR Power Equipment is recalling its lithium-ion battery-powered chainsaws due to potential fire and burn hazards. The affected models include 414170, 414181, 41418, and 414170R, identifiable by their black handles and orange buttons, along with a label stating DR Pulse 62V. The model and serial numbers can be found on a white label on the right side of the handle. It is crucial to handle the disposal of these chainsaws' lithium-ion batteries properly to prevent fire risks, as they should not be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, check with your local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: DR Power Equipment Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery Chainsaws Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DR Power at 800-940-6923 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recalls@DRPower.com, or visit http://drpower.com/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: Lithium-Ion Battery Chainsaws
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 7,180 (In addition, about 21 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Equipment battery-powered chainsaw (without battery)
Recalled DR Power Equipment battery-powered chainsaw (with battery)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of incidents of fire, sparks and/or smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent power equipment retailers nationwide and online at https://drpower.com from June 2018 to July 2024, priced between $200 and $350.

Importers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Overheating motor controller leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the chainsaws and request a pro-rated refund; proof of destruction required.

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls lithium-ion battery chainsaws due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact for a refund. Learn more about safe disposal.

recalls.Description: Kubota is recalling its Utility Tractors, specifically the model MX5400DTC, due to a potential crash hazard. These tractors feature an 8-speed gear drive transmission and a 55-horsepower Kubota 4-cylinder direct injection engine, all housed in a distinctive orange chassis with white 'KUBOTA' branding. The issue arises from a clutch cable that may break, leading to diminished braking capability.
recalls.Title: Kubota Utility Tractors Recalled for Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Kubota Tractor Corporation at 888-458-2682 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information is available through their contact form at https://www.kubotausa.com/recall/contact/form or by visiting https://www.kubotausa.com/recall/service-support/safety/safety-notices. Consumers can also find details on safety notices by navigating to the 'Parts & Service' section on www.kubotausa.com.

Products.Name: Kubota Utility Tractor MX5400DTC
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,600 (In addition, about 180 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Kubota Utility Tractor Model MX5400DTC with cab

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 33 reports of the clutch breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized Kubota dealers across the U.S. from February 2020 to December 2024, priced between $43,000 and $63,000.

Distributors.Name: Kubota Tractor Corporation, of Grapevine, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of crash due to potential clutch cable failure

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair through authorized Kubota dealers

recalls.metaDescription: Kubota recalls MX5400DTC Utility Tractors for crash risk due to clutch cable failure. Contact Kubota for free inspection and repair.

recalls.Description: Ariens is recalling specific models of their DELUXE, PLATINUM, and PROFESSIONAL-series Sno-Thro® snow throwers, which are identifiable by their bright orange color and either rear wheels or tracks. The model and serial numbers can be found on a white square ID tag located on the machine's frame. Some of these snow throwers have already undergone inspection and repair. Consumers are encouraged to visit https://www.ariensco.com/safetyrecall for a complete list of affected units.
recalls.Title: Ariens Snow Throwers Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Ariens Company at their toll-free number 877-740-7060, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. More information can also be found online at https://www.ariensco.com/safetyrecall or by visiting www.ariens.com and clicking on 'Safety Recalls.'

Products.Name: Ariens® DELUXE, PLATINUM, and PROFESSIONAL-series Snow Throwers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 6,260 (In addition, about 1,100 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Ariens Snow Throwers with Tracks
Recalled Ariens Snow Thrower with Wheels
Location of Model and Serial Number on the frame of the recalled Snow Throwers

Retailers.Name: Available through authorized Ariens dealers nationwide, as well as Ace Hardware, Blain's Farm & Fleet, Do It Best, Family Farm & Home, Mills Fleet Farm, Northern Tool and Equipment, Lowe's, and True Value. These products were sold online at AceHardware.com, Farmandfleet.com, Doitbest.com, familyfarmandhome.com, Lowes.com, fleetfarm.com, northerntool.com, and TrueValue.com from August 2024 to October 2024, priced between $1,770 and $5,440.

Hazards.Name: The impeller and auger assembly can keep rotating after the operator control lever is released, creating a laceration risk if a user comes into contact with the spinning parts inside the housing.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled snow throwers immediately and contact Ariens Company or an authorized dealer for a complimentary repair at their local dealership. Ariens and its authorized dealers will verify if a unit has been inspected or repaired and will conduct all necessary repairs on affected models.

recalls.metaDescription: Ariens recalls select snow throwers due to a laceration hazard. Stop using them immediately and contact Ariens for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Chervon North America is recalling SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries manufactured prior to May 1, 2021. These batteries, marked with "SKIL" and "PWRCORE 40," were sold individually under model BY8708-00 and in combination kits with SKIL 40V mowers. The recall specifically pertains to the battery only. Consumers can find the model number and manufacturing date code on a nameplate located on the top of the battery pack near the terminals, with affected date codes ranging from November 2019 to April 2021.
recalls.Title: Chervon North America Issues Recall for SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Chervon North America at 833-476-5325 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email BY8708-00recall@na.chervongroup.com, or visit www.skil.com/recalls for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Battery, model BY8708-00
Nameplate located on housing of the recalled battery shows model number and manufacturing date code (first 3 digits of serial number)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 100 reports of thermal incidents involving the batteries including overheating, melting, smoking and fire. These included eight reports of minor burns and/or smoke inhalation and 49 reports of related property damage.

Retailers.Name: Lowe's and various hardware and home improvement stores across the nation, as well as online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Lowes.com, from October 2019 through December 2024, priced at approximately $170 for individual batteries and $400 for combination kits with lawnmowers.

Importers.Name: Chervon North America Inc., of Naperville, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion batteries posing fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled batteries and contact Chervon for a free replacement or refund. Registration for the recall can be completed at www.skil.com/recalls, where Chervon will provide a collection kit for the return of the recalled battery.

recalls.metaDescription: Chervon recalls SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries due to fire risks. Learn how to get a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Outdoor Essentials is recalling its Cultivar planter boxes designed for gardening. These composite elevated planter boxes, measuring approximately 6 feet in length and 2.5 feet in width, have a capacity of 9.5 cu. ft. The model name can be found on the packaging, and it is listed as "70INX27IN Comp Elevated Planter" on purchase receipts. Consumers should be aware that the planter box frame may break, leading to potential injury.
recalls.Title: Outdoor Essentials Recalls Cultivar Planter Boxes Due to Injury Risk; Available Exclusively at Tractor Supply Company
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Outdoor Essentials at 888-891-8934, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at customerservice@outdooressentialproducts.com. Additional details can be found online at www.outdooressentialproducts.com/recall or by visiting https://www.outdooressentialproducts.com and selecting "Recall."

Images.Caption: Recalled Outdoor Essentials Cultivar Planter Box

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 23 reports of the planter box frame breaking, including one injury involving a consumer who broke their toes.

Manufacturers.Name: UFP Saginaw LLC, dba Outdoor Essentials, of Saginaw, Texas

Retailers.Name: Available at Tractor Supply Company stores nationwide and online at www.tractorsupply.com from December 2023 through September 2024 for approximately $300.

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to potential frame breakage

Remedies.Name: Stop using the planter boxes immediately and contact Outdoor Essentials for a full refund. Refunds can be issued as a gift card or store credit based on the purchase receipt. If no receipt is available, consumers will receive $275 for the model. Consumers must mark the recalled planter box with "Recalled" using a permanent marker and upload a photo to www.outdooressentialproducts.com/recall before disposing of the product.

recalls.metaDescription: Outdoor Essentials recalls Cultivar planter boxes due to injury risk. Learn how to get a refund and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description: John Deere has issued a recall for its 1023E, 1025R, and 2025R Compact Utility Tractors, identifiable by the model number and 'John Deere' logo on the hood. These tractors, available in green and yellow, were sold with both open and closed operator stations. To find the specific serial numbers affected by this recall, visit www.deere.com, navigate to 'Parts & Service,' and select 'Recall.' The serial number is located on the tractor's frame, on the front right side near the engine.
recalls.Title: John Deere Recalls 1023E, 1025R, and 2025R Compact Utility Tractors Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Deere & Company at 800-537-8233, available from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday to Friday, and 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET Saturday. Further details can be found online at www.deere.com by selecting 'Recalls' under 'Parts & Service' or by visiting https://www.deere.com/en/parts-and-service/recall-information/.

Products.Name: John Deere 1023E Compact Utility Tractor
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 147,900 (In addition, about 16,800 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Compact Utility Tractor

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of the brake linkage failing, including one hospitalization and two impact injuries and minor property damage to the tractors.

Retailers.Name: Authorized John Deere dealers across the nation, sold from November 2017 to July 2024, priced between $12,700 and $21,000.

Hazards.Name: Brake linkage front bell crank failure leading to loss of braking and crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled tractors and contact an authorized John Deere dealer for a complimentary repair appointment. If transport to the dealer is not feasible, arrangements will be made for the dealer to conduct repairs at the consumer's home. John Deere will also reach out directly to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: John Deere recalls 1023E, 1025R, and 2025R Compact Utility Tractors due to a crash hazard. Find out how to get your tractor repaired.

recalls.Description: Black & Decker has issued a recall for the CRAFTSMAN Model CMCTL320B V20 Cordless Tillers/Cultivators due to a potential laceration hazard. The model number can be found on the underside of the upper handle, and the product features a red and black design with the "CRAFTSMAN" logo on the handle and lower section. Only units with the UPC 885911843430 are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: CRAFTSMAN V20 Cordless Tillers/Cultivators Recalled by Black & Decker Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach CRAFTSMAN at 855-237-6848 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@sbdinc.com for updated assembly instructions. Additional information and downloadable instructions are available at https://www.craftsman.com/pages/safety-notices-and-recalls-cmctl320b or by visiting www.craftsman.com and selecting "Safety Notices and Recalls."

Images.Caption: Recalled CRAFTSMAN Model CMCTL320B V20 Cordless Tiller/Cultivator
Recalled CRAFTSMAN Model CMCTL320B V20 Cordless Tiller/Cultivator
Model Number on the underside of upper handle
UPC Code on product packaging
UPC Code

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of incorrect attachment of the bottom portion of the unit. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's, Ace Hardware, Blain's Farm & Fleet, select Army & Air Force Exchanges, and online at Amazon.com from October 2022 through April 2024, priced between $129 and $179.

Importers.Name: Black & Decker (U.S.) Inc., of Towson, Maryland

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to incorrect assembly instructions

Remedies.Name: Stop using the tillers/cultivators and contact CRAFTSMAN for updated assembly instructions. Instructions are also accessible online at CRAFTSMAN's website.

recalls.metaDescription: Black & Decker recalls CRAFTSMAN V20 Cordless Tillers due to laceration risk. Learn how to get updated assembly instructions.

recalls.Description: Oregon Tool has issued a recall for log splitters and cylinder kits under the brands Oregon, PowerPro, Country Tuff, and Speeco. This includes various models of log splitters, which can be identified by their brand names and model numbers located on the side of the units. Consumers should check the specific model numbers listed in the recall to determine if their product is affected.
recalls.Title: Oregon Tool Recalls Log Splitters and Cylinder Kits Due to Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Oregon Tool at 833-948-2590 from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email logsplitter@oregontool.com. More information is available online at https://www.oregontool.com/product-recalls/ or www.oregonproducts.com by clicking on 'Product Recalls'.

Products.Name: Country Tuff 25 Ton Log Splitter CT25TKO-1
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 29,560 (In addition, about 4,690 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Country Tuff 25 Ton Log Splitter

Injuries.Name: The firm has received seven reports of forward movement of the rod and attached splitting wedge at an unexpected increased speed. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's, Tractor Supply Company, and various other retailers across the nation, as well as online, from July 2020 to December 2023, priced between $1,500 and $2,745.

Importers.Name: Oregon Tool Inc., of Portland, Oregon

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to hydraulic cylinder rod separation from the piston, causing unexpected movement of the wedge.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the log splitters immediately if the wedge fails to retract and contact Oregon Tool or their dealer for a free replacement cylinder installation. It is advised not to attempt repairs independently. Dealers can be located by visiting www.oregonproducts.com/en/dealer-locator.

recalls.metaDescription: Oregon Tool recalls log splitters and cylinder kits due to injury risks. Check if your model is affected and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Ocean State Job Lot has issued a recall for the Calipso "Combi" and Tiller & Rowe "Growing Table-Mini Greenhouse and Raised Garden Bed" due to a potential fire hazard. These products, measuring 32" x 15" x 39" when assembled, feature an opaque cover with vents and a storage shelf beneath the garden bed. The legs and garden bed are gray, while the interior liner is lime green, complete with a removable grid for seedling spacing.
recalls.Title: Ocean State Job Lot Recalls Growing Table-Mini Greenhouse and Raised Garden Bed Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Ocean State Job Lot at 833-209-7100, available Monday to Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET and Saturday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or email customerservice@osjl.com. Home Lot customers can call 800-647-0783 or email support@mrpearl11.com.

Products.Name: Calipso Combi Growing Table-Mini Greenhouse and Raised Garden Bed

Images.Caption: Recalled Greenhouse
Recalled Greenhouse

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of the recalled greenhouses overheating, including one report of injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at Ocean State Job Lot stores in New England for $70 to $90 and through Home Lot online on platforms like Amazon, eBay, and Walmart from August 2019 to October 2023 for $100 to $150.

Importers.Name: Ocean State Jobbers Inc., of North Kingstown, Rhode Island

Distributors.Name: Ocean State Jobbers Inc., dba Ocean State Job Lot, of North Kingstown, Rhode Island

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately and contact Ocean State Job Lot for a full refund. Customers who bought from Ocean State Job Lot can return the item for a refund or call Customer Service for guidance. Home Lot customers must mark the product as "DO NOT USE" and dispose of it. They should send a photo of the marked product to Home Lot customer service at support@mrpearl11.com for a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Ocean State Job Lot recalls Growing Table-Mini Greenhouse due to fire risk. Learn how to get a refund and stay safe.

recalls.Description: Husqvarna is recalling its gas-powered Grass Trimmers, specifically models 330LK, 130C, and 130L. These trimmers are identifiable by their orange and gray color scheme, with the brand name and model number displayed on the motor housing. The SKU numbers for the affected models can be found on the serial number tag located on the underside of the motor housing, including various SKUs for each model listed in the recall.
recalls.Title: Husqvarna Grass Trimmers Recalled Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Husqvarna at 877-257-6921 from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recalls@husqvarnagroup.com, or visit www.husqvarna.com/us/recall. More information is available by clicking on 'Important Grass Trimmer Recall' on the homepage.

Products.Name: Husqvarna 330LK Grass Trimmer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 403,000 (In addition, about 17,230 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Gas-powered Grass Trimmer
Recalled Husqvarna Grass Trimmer (Model 130C)
Recalled Husqvarna Grass Trimmer (Model 130L)
Recalled Husqvarna Grass Trimmer (Model 330LK)
The SKU number is printed on the serial number tag on the bottom side of the motor housing

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 12 reports of the recalled grass trimmers catching on fire, electrical spark or arcing, including two reports of property damage and one report of a consumer who suffered burns on their leg and arm when they attempted to extinguish a fire.

Manufacturers.Name: Husqvarna Professional Products Inc., of Charlotte, N.C.

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized Husqvarna dealers, Lowe's, Tractor Supply, and various home and garden stores across the country, as well as online at Amazon.com from October 2021 through November 2023, priced between $250 and $300.

Hazards.Name: Electrical sparks or arcing due to incorrect wiring in the ignition module can create a fire hazard when gas is present.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the trimmers immediately and contact an authorized Husqvarna dealer for a free repair. A dealer locator is available at https://locations.husqvarna.com/us/.

recalls.metaDescription: Husqvarna recalls gas-powered Grass Trimmers due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for safety.
